what_it_does: Implements a generic, data-driven renderer (DNA.renderArchetype) that turns an archetype layout record and a data record into a DOM decision-card element, plus a composed slide renderer (DNA.renderExplained) that embeds a mockup alongside explanatory content.
observations: Defines DNA.renderArchetype and DNA.renderExplained on the global DNA namespace.
Uses a slot_map or slot.id to resolve values from a flattened record, including legibility fields and 'resolve:<name>' live async values.
Renders slots by (accepts-type, role) into design-system classes such as .p-h, .p-frost, .p-rows, and .p-glass.
The options slot supports two form factors: full-tile button for two or fewer options and ledger row for three or more options, with an ordinal metal ramp and a recommended badge.
Includes full-card state moments for empty and failed states with centered marks and retry buttons.
renderExplained builds a composed view that auto-selects a binary two-panel comparison, an n-option grid, or a default interface-plus-slide layout, with an optional grounded explanation region.
Wires option clicks to window.galleryBinder.decide(address, label) and emits CustomEvents for skip and retry actions.
Resolves live DNA tokens and grammar by fetching /dna/tokens.json and /dna/grammar.json once and applying DNA.injectVars and DNA.injectSpace.
Inlines a self-contained VI_MARK SVG so the V signature renders without requiring unit-view.js.
Suppresses the boilerplate 'a decision to make' in the what-kind label and applies length-aware question classes.
standouts: A single generic renderer intended to replace per-archetype renderers.
renderExplained produces source-faithful binary and n-option comparison panels with inline SVG visuals and live CTAs.
Async 'resolve:' slots stream into the DOM with a streaming cursor class.
conventions: Immediately-invoked function expression attaching to the global DNA namespace.
Functional helpers over classes; HTML built with template strings and esc() escaping.
Promise-cached token and grammar fetch to avoid repeated network calls.
onclick attributes injected as strings for event binding.
concerns: Inline onclick string construction mixes presentation with behavior and relies on a global window.galleryBinder.
Hard-coded fetch URLs (/dna/tokens.json, /dna/grammar.json) with minimal error handling.
Tight point-extraction regexes are brittle and may split on punctuation inside numbers or abbreviations.
renderExplained mixes three distinct layout modes and inline SVG generation, making it long.
The 'resolve:' placeholder '…' is returned synchronously while the async value streams later, which may cause content jump.
No validation of archetype or record shapes; missing fields silently produce empty strings.
notes: This is the shared runtime renderer that turns layout archetypes and decision records into DOM cards and composed slide views.
questions: What does DNA.org provide and which file loads it?
What is the exact schema of /dna/tokens.json and /dna/grammar.json?
How are archetype records generated and validated?
What is the expected lifecycle of opts.resolve promises and cancellation?
Which CSS file defines .dv-*, .ar-*, and .dc-* classes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the generic archetype renderer and composed explained view described in the header.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for rendering.
Reversible, non-binding decisions until explicitly taken.
intention_signals: The file is trying to unify all decision-card rendering under one generic, layout-driven renderer while also producing presentation-quality composed slides that contain the live interface and explain it side-by-side.
novelty: The combination of a generic archetype renderer with a composed 'explained' view that auto-selects binary versus n-option comparison layouts and embeds live take affordances inside presentation panels.
contribution: A reusable, data-driven rendering layer that separates layout archetypes from content records and provides both standalone card rendering and composed slide packaging.
summary_for_embedding: A generic JavaScript renderer that builds decision cards and composed explanatory slides from archetype layouts and data records, using design-system CSS classes, live async slot resolution, and global galleryBinder event wiring.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single shared runtime renderer for decision surfaces and gallery specimens so the same archetype-driven card can appear in the gallery, standalone pieces, and live decision surfaces.

symbols.does: Retrieves a nested value from an object by dotted path.
symbols.description: Splits the path on dots and reduces through the object, returning undefined if any intermediate key is missing. Used to read record fields specified by slot sources.

symbols.does: Determines the source path or identifier for a slot.
symbols.description: Returns the slot_map entry for the slot id, falling back to slot.from or slot.id, so the renderer knows which record field supplies the slot value.

symbols.does: Renders a single slot's value into HTML based on its type and role.
symbols.description: Dispatches on accepts-type and role to produce design-system markup; handles heading/question length-aware classes, what-kind suppression, explanation cards with an optional why line, lists, options, and shape/visual-device injection.

symbols.does: Builds the options control for a decision card.
symbols.description: Chooses between tile and row layouts depending on option count, renders state banners, a decided gold moment, a change header, and an optional 'Not now' ghost button, wiring data-decision and data-option attributes.

symbols.does: Renders a full-tile option button for binary decisions.
symbols.description: Emits a button with label, optional recommended or chosen badges, description, implication, and an explicit 'Choose' verb, marking the selected state.

symbols.does: Renders a ledger-row option button for three or more options.
symbols.description: Emits an ordinal-letter row with a position-driven metal ramp class, label, description, implication, check ring, and recommended or chosen badges.

symbols.does: Renders a zone and its slots into HTML.
symbols.description: Walks a zone's slots, resolves each value, wraps the result in a slot div honoring declared surface classes and focal marking, and emits a design-system product header for the head zone.

symbols.does: Renders full-card empty or failed state moments.
symbols.description: Returns a centered mark, heading, subline, and retry button for 'empty' or 'failed' card states.

symbols.does: Helper to fetch an icon from DNA.org.
symbols.description: Calls org.icon with name, size 16, and gold color if available; otherwise returns an empty string.

symbols.does: Builds the grounded-explanation region for composed views.
symbols.description: Resolves an explanation synchronously or asynchronously, emits a 'What this is' voice card with a streaming placeholder, and returns a wire function that updates the DOM when the promise resolves.

symbols.does: Attaches async explanation resolution to a rendered root.
symbols.description: Waits for the explanation promise and updates the explanation body and appends why_it_matters and grounding_note elements, removing the streaming class.

symbols.does: SVG helper drawing a single design-library tile.
symbols.description: Returns an SVG rect with two muted lines and an optional gold plus sign, used in the binary comparison visual.

symbols.does: SVG helper drawing a grid of design-library tiles.
symbols.description: Loops rows and columns to build a 2x3 tile grid, highlighting the last tile when requested.

symbols.does: SVG helper drawing a user icon.
symbols.description: Returns a circle head and shoulder arc path in the given color for the binary comparison flow visuals.

symbols.does: SVG helper drawing a centered caption text element.
symbols.description: Returns an SVG text node positioned at y=99 with a sans-serif font, used to label comparison visuals.

symbols.does: Splits an implication string into short bullet points for binary panels.
symbols.description: Splits on punctuation delimiters, trims trailing punctuation, filters short fragments, and limits to three points.

symbols.does: Renders one side of the binary comparison panel.
symbols.description: Builds a panel with a tag, heading, inline SVG visual, tight implication bullets, and a CTA button, applying recommended styling when favored.

symbols.does: Splits an implication string into short bullet points for n-option panels.
symbols.description: Uses the same splitting logic as tightPts but limits to two points for the denser n-option grid.

symbols.does: Renders one option panel in the n-option comparison grid.
symbols.description: Builds a panel with an ordinal or recommended tag, label, optional description, implication bullets, and a CTA button wired to the decision address.

symbols.does: Selects an ordinal metal color class for an option.
symbols.description: Returns r-gold for the recommended option, otherwise steps through ochre, bronze, and faint based on index.

symbols.does: Applies live DNA token and grammar variables to a rendered element.
symbols.description: Uses DNA.injectVars and DNA.injectSpace if present, applying caller-supplied tokens and grammar or fetching them once from /dna/tokens.json and /dna/grammar.json.

symbols.does: Inner helper that runs injectVars and injectSpace on an element.
symbols.description: Catches exceptions so missing surface functions do not break rendering; applies warmth and polarity tokens and density grammar.

symbols.does: Resolves a slot's value from the record or live resolver.
symbols.description: Handles 'resolve:' sources by calling opts.resolve and returning a placeholder for pending promises, otherwise reads from the flattened record or dotted path.
